task_completion.description: Vital Recorder is switched to Monitor mode
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Vital Recorder interface shows the 'Monitor' view active, typically indicated by a selected tab, button, or menu option for Monitor mode.

task_completion.description: Numeric vital signs are displayed on the screen
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main display area of Vital Recorder shows large numeric values for vital signs (e.g., HR, SpO2, Pressures) characteristic of a patient monitor, rather than just waveform graphs.

task_completion.description: The export or save dialog is opened to save the CSV
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file dialog window is open in Vital Recorder, showing the intent to save or export the recording data with the .csv extension.

task_completion.description: The CSV file is saved to the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: An icon for 'case_0003_review.csv' is visible on the Desktop background, or a file explorer window shows this file present in C:\Users\Docker\Desktop.

task_completion.description: The screenshot file is saved to the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: An icon for 'monitor_view_0003.png' is visible on the Desktop background, or a file explorer window shows this file present in C:\Users\Docker\Desktop.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent used the Vital Recorder GUI to export the CSV
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with Vital Recorder's menus (e.g., File -> Export) rather than using terminal commands to generate the CSV file.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent actually captured a screenshot of the screen
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses a screenshot tool, python script (e.g., pyautogui/PIL), or OS shortcut to capture the screen while Vital Recorder is visible in Monitor mode, rather than creating a dummy .png file.

integrity.description: Verifies the output files are not empty dummy files created via terminal
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal/command prompt should not show commands like 'touch case_0003_review.csv' or 'echo > monitor_view_0003.png' being used to bypass the actual application tasks.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Case 0003.vital is a 1h 13m 14s recording from the VitalDB dataset. It contains 81 tracks including ECG_II, ECG_V5, PLETH, COMPLIANCE, INSP_SEVO, and EXP_SEVO (often prefixed with SNUADC/ or Primus/). The expected outputs are a CSV file named 'case_0003_review.csv' and an image file named 'monitor_view_0003.png' located on the Desktop (C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\).
